title: with_ecc_key closure fixed to Error — breaks callers with their own error types
description: Scenario: Device::with_ecc_key<F, T> requires F: FnOnce(&mut EccKey<'_>) -> Result<T, Error>. Callers that use their own error types inside the closure must call .map_err(Into::into) or convert manually. Once this API is published, changing the bound to a generic E is a breaking change. Users doing anything non-trivial with the signing key (e.g. integrating with a larger application error type) will be forced to use From/Into conversions at every call site. Maintenance consequence: a common pattern in Rust scoped-resource APIs (see r2d2's Pool::run, deadpool) is to be generic over E. Fix before 1.0: change signature to 'where F: FnOnce(&mut EccKey<'_>) -> Result<T, E>, Error: Into<E>' or use a separate error enum, so callers control the error type.
notes: Requires a breaking API change before 1.0: change 'F: FnOnce(...) -> Result<T, Error>' to be generic over E. Defer to pre-1.0 API design pass alongside other API surface decisions.
